name: Serra Calderona Park: Pico Tristán Loop
name_original: Parque Natural de la Serra Calderona — Circular al Pico Tristán desde Estivella
description: This difficult 33-mile mountain bike route to Pico Tristán from Estivella offers panoramic views in Serra Calderona Natural Park.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Estivella offers diverse terrain within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park, characterized by verdant peaks, rugged rocks, and extensive trail networks. The region features a blend of hills, forests, and mountains, providing varied landscapes for cyclists. Notable features include the rocky ledge of El Garbí and the natural spring of Font del Barraix. This area provides a challenging environment for mountain bikers, with routes often involving significant elevation gains. Best mountain bike trails around Estivella The most popular mountain bike trail is Serra Calderona Natural Park - Circular to Pico Tristán from Estivella, a 33.1 miles (53.2 km) trail that takes 5 hours 19 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the natural park with substantial elevation gain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Ojos Negros Greenway – Historic center of Jérica loop from Estivella - Albalat dels Tarongers, a difficult 48.7 miles (78.4 km) path. This route combines sections of the Ojos Negros Greenway with a visit to the historic center of Jérica. Local mountain bikers also love the La Catalana Trail – Trail Along Barranco Codobal loop from Estivella - Albalat dels Tarongers, a 23.1 miles (37.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain including the Barranco Codobal, often completed in about 3 hours 10 minutes. Mountain biking around Estivella is defined by mountainous terrain, dense forests, and rugged rocks, with trails traversing the Sierra Calderona Natural Park. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Estivella are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 240 reviews. More than 3900 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Estivella's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Estivella?
A: There are over 215 mountain bike trails around Estivella, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Estivella?
A: Mountain biking in Estivella primarily features mountainous terrain with rugged rocks and dense, leafy plant life, especially within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park. You'll encounter a harmonious blend of hills, forests, and mountains, providing a visually engaging backdrop for your rides. Some routes may involve significant elevation gains and technical sections.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain biking in Estivella?
A: The trails around Estivella cater to various skill levels. While there are 32 easy routes, the majority are moderate (107 routes) to difficult (76 routes), often involving substantial elevation gains. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e908705340/serra-calderona-natural-park-circular-to-pico-tristan-from-estivella" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra Calderona Natural Park - Circular to Pico Tristán from Estivella&lt;/a&gt; are considered difficult due to their length and ascent.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints to see while mountain biking in Estivella?
A: Yes, the region is rich in natural and historical landmarks. A popular spot is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1054060" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garbí Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering breathtaking panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea. You might also encounter the natural spring of Font del Barraix, a refreshing stop. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos Negros Greenway&lt;/a&gt; is another significant feature, providing a unique cycling experience.

Q: Can I explore any historical sites or natural caves near the mountain bike trails?
A: Absolutely. Beyond the natural beauty, you can discover historical remnants like the architectural vestiges of Beselga Castle and the Els Arcs aqueduct in Estivella village. For those interested in caves, there are several nearby, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4079679" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Las Cuevas Calientes&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5352202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Inés Cave&lt;/a&gt;, which are accessible from various points in the region.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes around Estivella?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, the area offers extensive options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10384121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos Negros Greenway – Historic center of Jérica loop from Estivella - Albalat dels Tarongers&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 48.7-mile (78.4 km) path that combines sections of the historic greenway with a visit to Jérica. Another long route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e908705340/serra-calderona-natural-park-circular-to-pico-tristan-from-estivella" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra Calderona Natural Park - Circular to Pico Tristán from Estivella&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 33.1 miles (53.2 km).

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Estivella?
A: While many trails are moderate to difficult, there are 32 routes classified as easy around Estivella. These trails offer a gentler introduction to mountain biking in the region, allowing beginners to enjoy the natural scenery without overly challenging terrain.

Q: Are there mountain bike loop trails available in Estivella?
A: Many of the mountain bike routes around Estivella are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31472597" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Catalana Trail – Trail Along Barranco Codobal loop from Estivella - Albalat dels Tarongers&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14832713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font del Comte – Oronet Pass loop from Port de l'Oronet&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Estivella?
A: The region is generally suitable for mountain biking for much of the year. However, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer. The diverse ecosystems and verdant peaks are particularly beautiful during these seasons.

Q: Is parking available for mountain bikers in Estivella?
A: Yes, Estivella and its surrounding areas, being a popular outdoor destination, typically offer various parking options near trailheads and within the village itself. It's advisable to check specific starting points for routes for the most convenient parking.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Estivella?
A: The mountain bike trails around Estivella are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 240 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning natural beauty of the Sierra Calderona Natural Park, and the extensive network of trails that cater to various fitness levels. Many appreciate the challenging climbs and rewarding panoramic views, especially from spots like El Garbí.

Q: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the mountain bike trails in Estivella?
A: Estivella village itself features local amenities including cafes and restaurants where cyclists can refuel and relax after their rides. Given its location within a popular natural park, you may also find establishments in nearby towns or along main routes that cater to outdoor enthusiasts.
tips: Also known as the "Crestas del Conde", these are very curious caves that usually go unnoticed unless you look up. Their history is unknown but it is believed that they were used a long time ago for shelter and mainly as a storage place.
Site with panoramic views towards El Navajo Blanco and with good visibility you can see as far as the Mediterranean Sea. It is reached by forest tracks with a very good surface for rolling.

Getting to the top of Pico Tristán is a nice challenge no matter what path you take. From the top you will have incredible 360 degree views towards Valencia and surroundings. The last meters of the steep ascent to the forestry hut are concreted.
Very curious rock formation located on the Montemayor peak of 898m above sea level. They are noticeable from the road and draw a lot of attention
From there you can also continue a little more towards the antenna and appreciate the panoramic views

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is considered a difficult mountain bike ride. It demands a very good fitness level due to its length and elevation gain, and you should be prepared for technically challenging segments, including a steep, partially concreted climb to Pico Tristán. Some parts might even require pushing your bike.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail in Estivella?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, you'll typically find parking options in or near the town of Estivella, which serves as the starting point for this circular route. Look for public parking areas suitable for trail access.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Serra Calderona Natural Park - Circular to Pico Tristán from Estivella trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed in the Serra Calderona Natural Park, but they must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Always practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to tackle this route is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. Temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, making the long and difficult ride more challenging, while winter might bring colder temperatures and potentially wet conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for the Parc Natural de la Serra Calderona?
faqs.answer: No, there are no entrance fees or specific permits required to access the Parc Natural de la Serra Calderona for mountain biking. However, remember that mountain biking is restricted to forestry tracks, and a speed limit of 30 km/h applies to protect the natural environment.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is rugged and varied, featuring dense Mediterranean forests, distinctive red sandstone cliffs, and unpaved, technically demanding segments. The final ascent to Pico Tristán is particularly steep and partially concreted.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights and viewpoints along the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable spots. Key highlights include the Pico Tristán summit, offering spectacular 360-degree views, and the Navajo Blanco (Sanahuja) viewpoint with expansive vistas. Other points of interest include the Montemayor rock formation, the Ruins of Las Cuevas Calientes, and the Rebalsadores Snow Well.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other trails. You'll find yourself on parts of 'De Valencia a Lisboa (Etapa 03)', 'Vía Verde Ojos Negros', 'El Camí IT5TR7 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana', and 'Estivella - Penya Roja', among others.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect from Pico Tristán?
faqs.answer: From the summit of Pico Tristán, you'll be rewarded with breathtaking 360-degree panoramic views. These vistas stretch across Valencia and its surroundings, including the glittering Mediterranean Sea and the rolling plains of the region.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations for mountain biking in Serra Calderona Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Yes, to preserve the natural environment, mountain biking is generally restricted to designated forestry tracks. Mountain paths and open fields are typically off-limits for bikes, and a speed limit of 30 km/h is enforced. Please respect these rules to help maintain the park's beauty.

faqs.question: Is there water available along the trail?
faqs.answer: It's advisable to carry sufficient water for the entire ride, especially given its length and difficulty. While there might be occasional natural springs or small villages along the broader park area, relying on them for hydration on this specific route is not recommended. Plan to be self-sufficient.
